era: Hip-Hop. E=6 -- pioneer generation, Nastymix Records, first commercial Seattle hip-hop. Nastymix trilogy (177-179). v1.49.317.

location.formative: Nastymix Records ecosystem. Sir Mix-a-Lot's posse. Central District hip-hop scene.
location.career: Kid Sensation (Eric Hines). Nastymix Records to Ichiban Records. 1988-2000s.

members.name: Kid Sensation
members.note: Central District. Nastymix Records. 'Back to Boom.' Featured on 'Posse on Broadway.'

associated_musicians.name: Sir Mix-a-Lot
associated_musicians.relationship: Label head, mentor, collaborator. Nastymix Records founder. Featured Kid Sensation on 'Posse on Broadway.'

theory_nodes.concept: Pioneer generation as E=6 -- first-generation commercial Seattle hip-hop. Regional hit power. Nastymix credential. Posse on Broadway founding member. Before infrastructure existed.
examples: 'Back to Boom' as regional hit on KUBE 93.
*Rollin' with Number One* (1990) -- early Seattle hip-hop album.
theory_nodes.description: Pioneer generation. E=6 through founding-era credentials.

genealogy.relationship: Kid Sensation at 177. Pioneer generation opens Nastymix trilogy.

theory_nodes.concept: Nastymix trilogy (177-179) -- three consecutive degrees on the same label. Kid Sensation, High Performance, Sir Mix-a-Lot. The label that built Seattle hip-hop.
examples: Three-degree label streak. First in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Nastymix trilogy. Three consecutive degrees. One label.

genealogy.relationship: Kid Sensation at 177. Opens the trilogy.

theory_nodes.concept: Central District as hip-hop origin -- the geographic heart of Black Seattle and the birthplace of Seattle hip-hop culture. Kid Sensation documented this geography.
examples: Central District as home base for early Seattle hip-hop.
theory_nodes.description: Central District. Geographic origin of Seattle hip-hop.

genealogy.relationship: Kid Sensation at 177. Central District documented.

cross_references.relationship: NASTYMIX FOUNDER! Mix-a-Lot founded Nastymix. Kid Sensation was protege. 'Posse on Broadway' connected them. Label head meets flagship MC.
NASTYMIX LABEL! Both on Nastymix Records. Kid Sensation from Seattle, High Performance from Tacoma. Same label, different cities.
SEATTLE HIP-HOP! Both documenting Seattle through hip-hop. Different eras (1988 vs 2000s), different sounds, same city as subject.
REGIONAL! High Performance (178) returns Tacoma. Kid Sensation (177) anchors Seattle side. Nastymix bridged both cities.
